provider_perception.description: For a single brand, return Q24 attribute ratings vs the all-brand field average. Shows where the brand leads or trails the competitive set on each of the 12 perceptions dimensions.
provider_perception.base_note: Shown base only. Only answered Q24 attribute ratings included. Field average spans all 16 brands with shown responses.
provider_perception.scale_note: focus_avg and field_avg: direct –3..+3 scale. gap_vs_field: positive = brand leads field average. Sorted by gap descending (strengths first).

provider_comparison.description: Head-to-head Q24 attribute rating between a focus brand and one named competitor. Returns gap per attribute (focus minus competitor).
provider_comparison.base_note: Shown base only. Only answered Q24 attribute ratings included. Both brands must have been shown to the respondent.
provider_comparison.scale_note: gap: focus_avg minus competitor_avg. Positive = focus brand leads on that attribute. Sorted by attribute_idx.

provider_momentum.description: For all brands, compute the delta between current-period TSP rating (Q1) and 2-year-future TSP rating (Q3). Positive momentum = buyers expect improvement.
provider_momentum.base_note: Shown base only. Respondents must have answered both tsp_rating_now and tsp_rating_2yrs_future.
provider_momentum.scale_note: avg_rating_now and avg_rating_future: code 1–7 maps to labels –3..+3. momentum is the delta in code units. Sorted by momentum descending.

segment_profile.description: For a single brand, break down purchase intent (Q27) across one profile dimension. Set segment_by to one of: industry, emp_count, seniority, maturity_band, decision_involvement.
segment_profile.base_note: Shown base only. Only answered purchase_intent responses included.
segment_profile.scale_note: avg_intent_code: 1–6 (labels 0–5). pct_high_intent: % with code >= high_intent_threshold (default 5, labels 4 or 5-Almost certainly). Sorted by avg_intent_code descending.

segment_difference.description: Compare focus brand purchase intent vs the market average across one profile dimension. Shows where the brand over- or under-indexes.
segment_difference.base_note: Shown base only. Market average is the mean of all brand avg_intent_code values within the segment, not weighted by shown count.
segment_difference.scale_note: gap: focus_avg minus market_avg in code units. Positive = focus brand outperforms field in this segment. Sorted by gap descending.

unmet_needs.description: Return Q17 unmet-needs verbatims matching segment and keyword filters. Sorted by char_count descending (richest responses first).
unmet_needs.base_note: Q17_UnmetNeeds verbatims only (one per respondent). analysis.themes and analysis.sentiment are null until NLP pass runs.
unmet_needs.scale_note: ai_maturity_code: 1–10. word_count and char_count are raw text metrics.

target_list.description: Return anonymised respondent profiles showing high purchase intent for focus_brand. Set competitor_brand to filter to current users of that competitor (displacement targeting).
target_list.base_note: No PII. respondent_uuid is a random survey panel identifier. is_current_user reflects S9 code=1 for the focus_brand.

white_space.description: Return not-answered rate by brand and attribute among shown respondents. High rates indicate attributes no brand has yet claimed in buyers' minds — perception opportunities.
white_space.base_note: Shown base only. pct_not_answered includes only respondents who saw the brand (shown=true) but did not answer the attribute. Excludes not_shown.
white_space.scale_note: pct_not_answered: 0–100. Sorted descending. High value = unclaimed perception space.
